# Test basic mcptrace-to-otel functionality

# Create a simple trace file
exec cat > test.mcp << 'EOF'
# mcptrace:v1
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","id":1} # 1683000000.000 spanid=aaa1
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":"pong"} # 1683000000.100 spanid=aaa2
EOF

# Convert to stdout
exec mcptrace-to-otel -f test.mcp -type stdout
stdout 'Name.*mcp.recv.ping'
stdout 'Name.*mcp.send'
stdout 'Attributes:'
stdout 'mcp.direction.*recv'
stdout 'rpc.method.*ping'

# Test with trace context
exec cat > traced.mcp << 'EOF'
# mcptrace:v1 traceparent=00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-01
mcp-recv {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"test","id":1} # 1683000000.000
mcp-send {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":"ok"} # 1683000000.100
EOF

exec mcptrace-to-otel -f traced.mcp -type stdout
stdout 'mcp-trace'  # Root span name
stdout 'mcp.recv.test'
stdout 'mcp.send'
